headline: INPEX spuds Japan's first offshore CO₂-storage appraisal well off Chiba
summary: INPEX (85%) and Kanto Natural Gas Development (15%), via their Metropolitan CCS JV, began drilling the Kujukuri-Oki J-1 appraisal well ~5 km off Chiba — Japan's first offshore CO₂-storage appraisal well. Initial 1.3 Mtpa injection scalable to 5 Mtpa; FID targeted 2027, storage early 2030s.
co2crc_note: First offshore CO₂-storage appraisal well in Japan; anchors the Japan–APAC storage build-out relevant to Australia–Japan CCS value chains.

headline: Fugro wins Saipem survey contract for UK's first offshore CO₂ pipeline (Northern Endurance)
summary: Fugro contracted by Saipem for integrated marine survey and inspection along the 145-km NEP offshore CO₂ pipeline to the Endurance saline aquifer (up to ~1 Gt capacity). NEP is the bp/Equinor/TotalEnergies JV; UK's first offshore CO₂ T&S, storage targeted 2028.
co2crc_note: Supply-chain milestone on the UK's first offshore CO₂ T&S network — a reference model for Australian offshore storage T&S.
extractor_note: Up to ~1 Gt is cumulative storage capacity, not annual — capacity_mtpa left null; contract value not disclosed.

headline: Shanghai body to launch China's first CO₂-removal standard
summary: QCIntel reports a Shanghai-based organisation is preparing to launch what would be China's first dedicated CO₂-removal (CDR) standard, building a domestic crediting rulebook for engineered/geological removals alongside the relaunched CCER scheme. Details limited behind paywall.
co2crc_note: Early signal of China building CDR crediting infrastructure — shapes the largest potential future demand pool for removals.
extractor_note: Launching body not named in available text; uncorroborated beyond source headline.

headline: Gasunie-commissioned PwC study finds CCS the cheapest route to decarbonise Dutch industry
summary: A PwC study for Gasunie concludes CCS, low-carbon hydrogen and hybrid electrification are the most cost-effective decarbonisation routes for Dutch industry: CCS modelled to abate ~21 Mt CO₂/yr (~18 Mt via blue hydrogen). Warns high energy costs and grid congestion delay investment.
co2crc_note: Independent techno-economic backing for CCS as least-cost industrial decarbonisation — a useful data point for Australian industrial-cluster policy.
extractor_note: 21 Mt CO₂/yr is a modelled abatement scenario, not project capacity — capacity_mtpa left null.

headline: IEEFA warns offshore CCS ambitions vastly outpace capability
summary: IEEFA report (Anika Juhn) argues offshore CO₂-storage ambitions run far ahead of operational reality — scaling from ~3.6 Mt today toward ~140 Mtpa targets is a ~40-fold expansion needing centuries of secure containment; today's operating base is insufficient. Cites Northern Lights' ~US$714m phase-2.
co2crc_note: Financial-risk critique of offshore storage scale-up — directly relevant to Australia's offshore CCS pipeline and its social-licence framing.

headline: Australian trade press asks "Is CCS just smoke and mirrors?"
summary: Energy News Bulletin runs a sceptical news-analysis questioning whether CCS delivers on its promises, reflecting continuing domestic social-licence pressure on the sector. Surfaced via Google Alerts; full text paywalled/unread.
co2crc_note: Australian-market sceptic framing — the home-turf social-licence narrative CO2CRC must engage with.
extractor_note: Headline-level only; article body not fetchable (proxy 403).
